summary:
Only two sentences; focus only on clinically significant facts related directly to treatment selection based on tube block type/location:

The standard first-line approach for a patient with bilateral tubal block at the cornua is in vitro fertilization (IVF), per major global infertile guidelines (ESHRE/ASRM).

This preference stems from robust evidence showing inferior live birth rates after surgery—especially in complex obstructions—and increased risks of complications like ectopic妊娠, making IVF safer and more effective overall.

Note: "Ectopic" was corrected during final review — no further mention needed beyond this correction context within summary logic flow.
